items.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explodes during testing in Florida
items.summary: The explosion is likely a major setback for Jeff Bezos' spaceflight company, and its attempt to compete with SpaceX.

items.title: Glean’s top line crosses $300M as AI budget-cutting becomes its major selling point
items.summary: The enterprise AI search startup tripled its annual revenue even as tech giants entered the category.

items.title: Waymo dominates autonomous vehicle registrations as Tesla trails behind
items.summary: A new law and AV tracker tool gives the clearest accounting yet of how many robotaxis and self-driving trucks are in Texas.

items.title: How to apply to Startup Battlefield 2026, what you need ahead of the June 8 deadline
items.summary: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

items.title: The internet is being rebuilt for machines
items.summary: As AI agents move from experiments to production, AWS, Cloudflare, and others are redesigning cloud infrastructure for a future dominated by machine-generated internet traffic instead of human users.

items.title: Slate Auto will announce pricing and take preorders for its EV on June 24
items.summary: The Bezos-backed EV startup has yet to announce final pricing for its vehicle, which is supposed to start shipping by the end of this year.

items.title: Asana acquires no-code agent-builder StackAI
items.summary: Asana will incorporate StackAI into its growing suite of AI workflow tools.

items.title: Bluesky embraces long-form content to counter X Articles
items.summary: In its latest update, Bluesky is getting into long-form content.

items.title: Hackers are trying to steal Signal users’ backups in new wave of phishing attacks
items.summary: A new hacking campaign is trying to trick Signal users to give up their secret recovery key, which can be used to access online backups containing past messages.

items.title: Anthropic raises $65 billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO
items.summary: Anthropic has closed a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, marking what could be the AI startup's final private fundraise before a highly anticipated IPO.

items.title: Just like gold and oil, we’ll soon be able to trade AI token futures
items.summary: Large exchanges are designing derivative products around AI tokens, which are increasingly being considered less a computational output and more a raw material input, like electricity or bandwidth.

items.title: H1 secures $40M from CVS, proving SaaS startups can still attract investment
items.summary: CEO Ariel Katz argues that while AI can replicate workflow SaaS, it can't copy H1’s unique doctor data.

items.title: A security lapse at prison pay phone service Pay Tel publicly exposed over 300K callers’ driver’s licenses
items.summary: Pay Tel secured the publicly exposed data after security researchers discovered the leak containing callers' sensitive ID documents and inmate communications.

items.title: Why Paris may be the most important AI city outside Silicon Valley
items.summary: Europe’s startup ecosystem has matured significantly; its founders are increasingly willing to scale companies domestically instead of immediately looking to relocate to the U.S.

items.title: In just 3 weeks, StrictlyVC is coming to Los Angeles
items.summary: StrictlyVC Los Angeles is on June 18. Join for meaningful networking and fireside chats with leaders from Mach Industries, Shinkei Systems, and more. Register today.

items.title: Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline extended to June 8 after overwhelming demand
items.summary: After overwhelming demand from founders around the world, TechCrunch has extended the Startup Battlefield 200 application deadline to June 8. Nominate a standout startup or apply yours today.

items.title: Corgi announces $106M raise at $2.6B valuation — double what it was worth 3 weeks ago
items.summary: While startups raising back-to-back rounds at steep step-ups have become almost routine, a company whose valuation doubles in three weeks is unusual enough to raise questions, particularly given the investor set in both rounds is the same.

items.title: Anthropic releases Opus 4.8 with new ‘dynamic workflow’ tool
items.summary: The new Opus model comes with a tool called Dynamic Workflows, for coordinating swarms of subagents.

items.title: Waymo’s newest robotaxi is Chinese-made, built to make money, and now accepting riders
items.summary: The launch of the Ojai minivan robotaxi comes after years of development and testing, but arrives amid a challenging time for Waymo.

items.title: US says troops were targeted with location data, as senator warns ad industry is a ‘national security threat’
items.summary: One leading privacy lawmaker said it was time to "start treating the adtech industry as a national security threat."
